|city= [[Abu Dhabi]], United Arab Emirates
|attendance= 2,600<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.eurosport.com/mixed-martial-arts/ufc-257-conor-mcgregor-ko-at-the-hands-of-dustin-poirier-leaves-lightweight-division-in-limbo_sto8081170/story.shtml|title=UFC 257 - Conor McGregor KO at the hands of Dustin Poirier leaves lightweight division in limbo|publisher=eurosport.com|author=Staff|date=2021-01-24|access-date=2021-01-24}}</ref>
|gate=
|buyrate= 1,504,737<ref>{{cite web |last1=King |first1=Nolan |title=Conor McGregor shares receipt, reveals massive UFC 257 pay-per-view buy rate |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/10/ufc-257-news-conor-mcgregor-pay-per-view-ppv-buy-rate |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |date=4 October 2021 |access-date=5 October 2021}}</ref>
|previousevent= [[UFC on ESPN: Chiesa vs. Magny]]
|followingevent= [[UFC Fight Night: Overeem vs. Volkov]]

'''''UFC 257: Poirier vs. McGregor 2''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on January 24, 2021 <!-- Local date in Abu Dhabi, do not change it!!!!--> at the [[Etihad Arena]] on [[Yas Island]], [[Abu Dhabi]], United Arab Emirates.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ufc.com/news/conor-mcgregor-vs-dustin-poirier-made-official-ufc-257|title=Dustin Poirier vs Conor McGregor to headline UFC 257|publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff|date=2020-11-23|access-date=2020-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/12/22/22195150/dana-white-announces-ufc-257-first-three-events-of-2021-to-take-place-on-fight-island|title=Dana White announces UFC 257, first three events of 2021 to take place on Fight Island|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2020-12-22|access-date=2020-12-22}}</ref>

==Background==
Starting with [[UFC on ABC: Holloway vs. Kattar]], a limited number of fans were allowed inside the newly built [[Etihad Arena]], marking the first time since [[UFC 248]] on March 7, 2020, that non-essential event personnel were in attendance.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/ufc-limited-fans-attendance-etihad-arena-fight-island-mma-conor-mcgregor-dustin-poirier|title=Fans are back: UFC will have 'limited' attendance at three-card 'Fight Island' stint at Etihad Arena|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2021-01-07|access-date=2021-01-07}}</ref> The venue has maximum capacity of over 18,000, but the UFC was expecting to have closer to 2,000 fans for each of the Fight Island's events during the week.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/1/10/22223333/etihad-arena-lists-official-covid-19-safety-measures-for-fans-at-january-ufc-fight-island-shows|title=Etihad Arena lists official COVID-19 safety measures for fans at January UFC Fight Island shows|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Newswire|date=2021-01-10|access-date=2021-01-11}}</ref>

For the event to be broadcast live during [[Prime time]] hours on the [[Eastern Time Zone|east coast]] of North America, the main card began at 7:00 am (January 24) local time in Abu Dhabi, with a full preliminary card beginning at approximately 4:00 am [[Gulf Standard Time]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/commentary-teams-broadcast-plans-set-for-three-event-stretch-on-fight-island-ending-with-ufc-257|title='Fight Island' commentary teams, broadcast plans set for three events ending with UFC 257|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2021-01-13|access-date=2021-01-13}}</ref>

A lightweight rematch between former [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight]] and [[UFC Lightweight Championship|Lightweight Champion]] [[Conor McGregor]] and former interim champion [[Dustin Poirier]] headlined this event.<ref name="Rematch">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/11/10/21558071/conor-mcgregor-vs-dustin-poirier-2-officially-booked-for-ufc-257|title=Conor McGregor vs. Dustin Poirier 2 in works for UFC 257|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2020-11-10|access-date=2020-11-11}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/conor-mcgregor-vs-dustin-poirier-rematch-booked-ufc-257|title=Conor McGregor vs. Dustin Poirier 2 booked for UFC 257|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura and Mike Bohn|date=2020-11-19|access-date=2020-11-19}}</ref> They met previously in a featherweight bout at [[UFC 178]] on September 27, 2014, where McGregor won by first-round TKO.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2014/09/ufc-178-results-photos-conor-mcgregor-stops-dustin-poirier-in-first-round|title=UFC 178 results, photos: Conor McGregor stops Dustin Poirier in first round|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2014-09-27|access-date=2020-11-11}}</ref>

After defending his lightweight title at [[UFC 254]] in October, [[Khabib Nurmagomedov]] immediately announced his retirement, citing his father's death from complications related to COVID-19 in last July as the main reason behind it.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/10/ufc-254-khabib-nurmagomedov-dominates-announces-retirement-ufc-254-justin-gaethje|title=Khabib Nurmagomedov dominates Justin Gaethje, retires after UFC 254 submission win|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Dave Doyle|date=2020-10-24|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref> Despite his announcement, the title was never officially vacated as UFC President [[Dana White]] said several times that he believed Nurmagomedov would still fight again.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/10/ufc-news-dana-white-thinks-khabib-nurmagomedov-wont-stay-retired|title=Dana White has 'a feeling' Khabib Nurmagomedov won't stay retired after talking to him|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Samano|date=2020-10-29|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/dana-white-door-open-khabib-nurmagomedov-return-ufc-video|title=Dana White leaves door open for Khabib Nurmagomedov to return: 'He's still the champ'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Head and Ken Hathaway|date=2020-11-01|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/dana-white-insists-conor-mcgregor-vs-dustin-poirier-not-for-the-title-khabib-nurmagomedov-will-fight-again-ufc|title=Dana White insists Conor McGregor vs. Dustin Poirier won't be for title: 'Khabib's going to fight'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun and John Morgan|date=2020-11-18|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref> A meeting between both of them to discuss the champion's future was then expected to happen this week during the organization's stint at [[Fight Island]], eventually taking place on January 15.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/ufc-news-dana-white-meeting-khabib-nurmagomedov-yes-or-no-retirement|title=Dana White anticipates Khabib Nurmagomedov meeting will be five minutes: 'It's yes or no'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and John Morgan|date=2021-01-13|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/ufc-news-dana-white-khabib-nurmagomedov-meeting-video-uae-warriors-abu-dhabi|title=Video: Dana White and Khabib Nurmagomedov finally meet face to face in Abu Dhabi|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2021-01-15|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref> The following day, White made an appearance live on [[American Broadcasting Company|ABC]] during the main card of [[UFC on ABC: Holloway vs. Kattar]] and revealed the outcome of the meeting: the Russian left the door open to a potential return, depending on how the main and co-main event fighters perform at this event, while also praising [[Charles Oliveira]]'s performance at [[UFC 256]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/ufc-on-abc-1-dana-white-to-reveal-khabib-nurmagomedov-decision|title=What's happening with Khabib Nurmagomedov? Dana White will reveal all at UFC on ABC 1|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Head, The Blue Corner|date=2021-01-16|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/1/16/22233411/dana-white-khabib-nurmagomedov-return-ufc-257-lightweights-charles-oliveira-can-do-something-special|title=Dana White announces that Khabib Nurmagomedov could return if UFC 257 lightweights, Charles Oliveira 'can do something special'|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2021-01-16|access-date=2021-01-16}}</ref> Despite White's announcement, Nurmagomedov indicated in another interview that "fighting again is not in my plans".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/1/20/22241353/khabib-nurmagomedov-fighting-not-in-my-plans-dont-push-me-to-do-things-that-will-disappoint-mother|title=Khabib Nurmagomedov: Fighting not in my plans, don't 'push me to do things that will disappoint my mother'|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Newswire|date=2021-01-20|access-date=2021-01-20}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between promotional newcomers [[Umar Nurmagomedov]] and [[Sergey Morozov (fighter)|Sergey Morozov]] was initially scheduled to take place at [[UFC 254]], but Nurmagomedov pulled out due to an illness.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/10/ufc-254-umar-nurmagomedov-ruled-out-octagon-debut|title=Umar Nurmagomedov hospitalized in Dubai, ruled out of octagon debut at UFC 254|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Head|date=2020-10-18|access-date=2020-11-24}}</ref> The pairing was then rescheduled for this event.<ref name="tryagain">{{Cite web|url=https://mmadna.nl/umar-nurmagomedov-vs-sergey-morozov-toegevoegd-aan-ufc-257-op-23-januari/|title=Umar Nurmagomedov vs. Sergey Morozov added to UFC 257 on January 23|publisher=mmadna.nl|author=Staff|date=2020-11-24|access-date=2020-11-24}} {{in lang|nl}}</ref> Finally, the bout was set to take place 3 days prior at [[UFC on ESPN: Chiesa vs. Magny]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/umar-nurmagomedov-sergey-morozov-ufc-on-espn-20-january-20|title=Umar Nurmagomedov vs. Sergey Morozov moved to UFC on ESPN 20 on Jan. 20|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2021-01-12|access-date=2021-01-12}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between [[Amir Albazi]] and [[Zhalgas Zhumagulov]] was expected to take place at [[UFC on ESPN: Smith vs. Clark]], but Zhumagulov pulled out due to [[travel visa|visa]] issues and the bout was rescheduled for this event.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/ufc-on-espn-18-amir-albazi-vs-zhalgas-zhumagulov-flyweight-passport-issues|title=Amir Albazi vs. Zhalgas Zhumagulov pulled from UFC on ESPN 18 lineup|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2020-11-24|access-date=2020-11-24}}</ref><ref name="AlbaziZhumagulov">{{cite web|url=http://mma.uno/amir-albazi-vs-zhalgas-zhumagulov-transferido-a-ufc-257/|title=Amir Albazi vs. Zhalgas Zhumagulov rescheduled for UFC 257|publisher=mma.uno|author=Steve Duncan|date=2020-11-25|access-date=2020-11-25}} {{in lang|es}}</ref>

Former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Atomweight Championship|Invicta FC Atomweight Champion]] [[Michelle Waterson]] was expected to face [[Amanda Ribas]] in a women's strawweight bout at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/30246866/michelle-waterson-amanda-ribas-closing-deal-fight-ufc-257-sources-say|title=Michelle Waterson, Amanda Ribas closing in on deal to fight at UFC 257, sources say|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2020-11-02|access-date=2020-12-08}}</ref> However, Waterson pulled out of the bout in early December due to undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/12/8/22164091/michelle-waterson-out-of-upcoming-fight-against-amanda-ribas-at-ufc-257|title=Michelle Waterson out of upcoming fight against Amanda Ribas at UFC 257|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin and Guilherme Cruz|date=2020-12-08|access-date=2020-12-08}}</ref> She was replaced by [[Marina Rodriguez (fighter)|Marina Rodriguez]].<ref name="MRodriguez">{{cite web|url=https://agfight.com/noticias/ufc-encaminha-duelo-entre-amanda-ribas-e-marina-rodriguez-para-janeiro/?doing_wp_cron=1607523611.0528929233551025390625|title=UFC books fight between Amanda Ribas and Marina Rodriguez for January|publisher=agfight.com|author=Diego Ribas|date=2020-12-08|access-date=2020-12-09}} {{in lang|pt}}</ref>

[[Tagir Ulanbekov]] was briefly scheduled to face returning veteran [[Matheus Nicolau]] in a flyweight bout.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/12/matheus-nicolau-vs-tagir-ulanbekov-ufc-257-mma-fight-booking-january-23|title=Matheus Nicolau returns vs. Tagir Ulanbekov at UFC 257 on Jan. 23|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2020-12-18|access-date=2020-12-21}}</ref> However, Ulanbekov withdrew in late December due to undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/12/19/22191093/matheus-nicolau-signs-ufc-needs-opponent-ufc-257-tagir-ulanbekov-out|title=Matheus Nicolau re-signs with UFC, needs new opponent for UFC 257 after Tagir Ulanbekov withdraws|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2020-12-19|access-date=2020-12-21}}</ref> The pairing was eventually rescheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Edwards vs. Chimaev]] on March 13.<ref name="BackAgain">{{cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/combate/noticia/ultimate-encaminha-remarcacao-de-matheus-nicolau-x-tagir-ulanbekov-para-13-de-marco.ghtml|title=Ultimate sends Matheus Nicolau vs. Tagir Ulanbekov to March 13|publisher=globoesporte.globo.com|author=Raphael Marinho|date=2021-01-12|access-date=2021-01-13}} {{in lang|pt}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Joanna vs. Team Cláudia]] light heavyweight winner [[Andrew Sanchez]] and [[André Muniz]] was expected to take place at this event.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/11/19/21575006/andre-muniz-tuf-23-winner-andrew-sanchez-ufc-257-mma|title=Andre Muniz set to face TUF 23 winner Andrew Sanchez at UFC 257|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz and Damon Martin|date=2020-11-19|access-date=2020-12-24}}</ref> In late December, Muniz withdrew due to an injury and was replaced by [[Makhmud Muradov]].<ref name="MuradovIn">{{cite web|url=https://sport.tn.nova.cz/clanek/mach-na-turnaji-s-mcgregorem-proti-komu-se-cesky-uzbek-postavi.html|title=Fight in the same event as McGregor! Who will the Czech/Uzbek oppose?|publisher=sport.tn.nova.cz|author=Richard Wagner|date=2020-12-24|access-date=2020-12-24|archive-date=2021-04-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210416080718/https://sport.tn.nova.cz/clanek/mach-na-turnaji-s-mcgregorem-proti-komu-se-cesky-uzbek-postavi.html|url-status=dead}} {{in lang|cs}}</ref>

A women's bantamweight bout between [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Rousey vs. Team Tate]] bantamweight winner [[Julianna Peña]] and former [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] challenger  [[Sara McMann]] (also [[Wrestling at the 2004 Summer Olympics|2004 Olympic silver medalist]] in wrestling) was originally scheduled to take place a week earlier at [[UFC on ABC: Holloway vs. Kattar]], but it was eventually pushed back to this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/ufc-fight-night-sara-mcmann-julianna-pena-january-16|title=Sara McMann vs. Julianna Pena added to UFC Fight Night on Jan. 16|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2020-11-19|access-date=2020-12-26}}</ref><ref name="PeñaMcMann">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/12/ufc-257-news-julianna-pena-vs-sara-mcmann-added-booking|title=UFC 257 gets Julianna Pena vs. Sara McMann after fight pushed back one week|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and Mike Bohn|date=2020-12-26|access-date=2020-12-26}}</ref>

A featherweight bout between [[Shane Burgos]] and [[Hakeem Dawodu]] was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Burgos-vs-Dawodu-Nurmagomedov-vs-Morozov-Booked-for-UFC-257-176400|title=Shane Burgos vs. Hakeem Dawodu booked for UFC 257|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Tristen Critchfield|date=2020-11-24|access-date=2021-01-10}}</ref> However, on January 9, Dawodu was forced to withdraw from the bout citing a shoulder injury.<ref name="Dawodu out">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/ufc257-hakeem-dawodu-ruled-out-shane-burgos-fight|title=Injury forces Hakeem Dawodu out of UFC 257 clash with Shane Burgos|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Head and Farah Hannoun|date=2021-01-09|access-date=2021-01-10}}</ref> The UFC confirmed three days later that Burgos was also injured and the bout was canceled.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ufc.com/news/updates-ufc-fight-island-events-read|title=Updates to UFC Fight Island events|publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff|date=2021-01-12|access-date=2021-01-12}}</ref>

On the day of the weigh-ins, a couple of lightweight bouts suffered major changes due to several issues: [[Ottman Azaitar]] and [[Matt Frevola]] were scheduled to meet in the preliminary portion of the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/ufc-257-ottman-azaitar-vs-matt-frevola-booked|title=Ottman Azaitar vs. Matt Frevola joins UFC 257 lineup|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun and John Morgan|date=2020-11-19|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref> However, it was announced that Azaitar was pulled from the bout and had his contract terminated after it was determined that he had violated COVID-19 health and safety protocols, as he attempted to help others enter the UFC's designated safety zone.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/1/22/22244418/ottman-azaitar-cut-from-ufc-after-violating-covid-19-safety-protocol|title=Ottman Azaitar cut from UFC after violating COVID-19 safety protocol|publisher=mmafighting.usatoday.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2021-01-22|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref> The other bout that suffered alterations featured [[Nasrat Haqparast]] and [[Arman Tsarukyan]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/ufc-257-nasrat-haqparast-vs-arman-tsarukyan-booked|title=Nasrat Haqparast vs. Arman Tsarukyan added to UFC 257|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2020-11-19|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref> Haqparast withdrew from the bout due to an undisclosed illness and Tsarukyan weighed in at 157 pounds, one pound over the lightweight non-title fight limit.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2021/01/22/nasrat-haqparast-ufc-257-fight-arman-tsarukyan-missed-weight/|title=Nasrat Haqparast out of UFC 257 fight with Arman Tsarukyan, who missed weight|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2021-01-22|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/1/22/22244033/ufc-257-weigh-in-results-conor-mcgregor-dustin-poirier-make-weight-for-rematch-two-fights-canceled|title=UFC 257 weigh-in results: Conor McGregor, Dustin Poirier make weight for rematch, two other fights canceled|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2021-01-22|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref> As a result, the promotion arranged a catchweight bout between Frevola and Tsarukyan to keep both athletes on the card.<ref name="changes">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/01/ufc-257-conor-mcgregor-dustin-poirier-weigh-in-results-live-video-stream|title=UFC 257 weigh-in results: Lineup altered after multiple issues with lightweights|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2021-01-22|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref><ref name="newfight">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/1/22/22244225/arman-tsarukyan-vs-matt-frevola-arranged-for-ufc-257-following-loss-of-scheduled-opponents|title=Arman Tsarukyan vs. Matt Frevola arranged for UFC 257 following loss of scheduled opponents|publisher=mmafighting.usatoday.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2021-01-22|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref> Subsequently, Tsarukyan forfeited 20% of his purse to Frevola as a result of missing weight.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://empiresportsmedia.com/mma/matt-frevola-will-now-meet-arman-tsarukyan-at-ufc-257/|title=Matt Frevola will now meet Arman Tsarukyan at UFC 257|publisher=empiresportsmedia.com|author=Daniel Cunningham|date=2021-01-22|access-date=2021-01-22}}</ref>
